title: copier: use `PAX` format instead of default `USTAR`
body:
File copied to container from host does not preserves `mtime` correctly because `USTAR` format only supports precision of 1 second, switch to `PAX` format which typically supports Nanosecond precision.

Helpful in RUN-2664: `Ensure that Buildah instructions that copy files do not make any changes`

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [rhatdan](https://github.com/rhatdan) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
We try to not force PAX format unless we absolutely have to, since it makes the layer diff larger.  I'm inclined to reject this.
Inside container buildkit retains nanosecond precision for `mtime`, I am suspecting that buildkit is using `PAX` format by default. But I need to confirm this.

The header format used in an image's layer blobs can control how much precision there is the layer blob, and whether or not ctime and atime are included.  A container's contents sit on a regular filesystem, and those keep timestamps with levels of precision that vary depending on which filesystem is being used.  We really shouldn't be assuming anything about the latter.

Yes, I also found a example in buildkit which created `USTAR` tar so my assumption was wrong.

```
FROM scratch
COPY somefile .
```

created a layer with `USTAR` .

I'll open a new PR if I find any other method I'm closing this one now.
